question: Among NYC municipal candidates who had total campaign spending between $103,000 and $251,000 in each of the five election cycles 2001, 2003, 2005, 2009, and 2013, find the candidate that represented the borough with the lower number of residents living in New York City public housing who were accepted and enrolled in the city's summer jobs program for young people in 2019. How many years did that candidate serve in the office of a New York City Council member before his election to the NYC Council? Return the number only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2001-campaign-expenditures/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

result = (
    df.groupby(["CANDID", "CANDFIRST", "CANDLAST"], dropna=False, as_index=False)["AMNT"]
      .sum()
      .rename(columns={"AMNT": "total_amnt"})
)
result = result[(result["total_amnt"] >= 103000) & (result["total_amnt"] <= 251000)]
result = result.sort_values(["CANDLAST", "CANDFIRST", "CANDID"], kind="stable").reset_index(drop=True)
answer = (result["CANDFIRST"].astype(str).str.strip() + " " + result["CANDLAST"].astype(str).str.strip()).tolist()
1.subquestion: Which candidates had total campaign spendings between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2001 election cycle?

6.fact: He represents district 14 in the New York State Senate, which comprises St. Albans, Cambria Heights, Jamaica, Hollis, Rosedale, Laurelton, Kew Gardens, Queens Village and other neighborhoods within the borough of Queens.
6.subquestion: Which NYC borough did Leroy Comrie represent?

7.fact: Recchia formerly represented the 47th district of the New York City Council, which included areas of Bensonhurst, Brighton Beach, Coney Island, and Gravesend in south Brooklyn.
7.subquestion: Which NYC borough did Domenic Recchia represent?

8.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/summer-youth-employment-program-syep-for-nycha-residents-by-borough-local-law-163/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
node_1_answer = "Leroy Comrie; Domenic Recchia"
node_2_answer = "Queens; Brooklyn"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["Year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce")
df["Were accepted and enrolled"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["Were accepted and enrolled"].astype(str).str.replace(",", "", regex=False),
    errors="coerce"
)

candidates = [part.strip() for part in node_1_answer.split(";")]
boroughs = [part.strip() for part in node_2_answer.split(";")]
candidate_by_borough = dict(zip(boroughs, candidates))
result = df[
    (df["Year"] == 2019)
    & (df["Borough"].isin(boroughs))
][["Borough", "Were accepted and enrolled"]].sort_values(
    ["Were accepted and enrolled", "Borough"], ascending=[True, True], kind="stable"
).reset_index(drop=True)
selected_borough = result.iloc[0]["Borough"]
answer = candidate_by_borough[selected_borough]
8.subquestion: Among the candidates from <hop 1 answer>, which candidate represented the borough with the lower number of residents living in New York City public housing who were accepted and enrolled in the city's summer jobs program for young people in 2019?

9.fact: Prior to his election to the NYC Council, Comrie served seven years in the office of New York City Council Member Archie Spigner, moving from an aide to Chief of Staff during his tenure. He has also served as a member and eventually president of Community School Board 29.
9.subquestion: How many years did <hop 3 answer> serve in the office of a New York City Council member before his election to the NYC Council?

Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer>, <node 2 answer>, <node 3 answer>, <node 4 answer>, and <node 5 answer>, ordered by last name, = Leroy Comrie; Domenic Recchia.
Node 6: He represents district 14 in the New York State Senate, which comprises neighborhoods within the borough of Queens.
Node 7: Recchia formerly represented the 47th district of the New York City Council, which included areas in south Brooklyn.
Node 8: Compare 2019 accepted-and-enrolled counts for the boroughs from <hop 2 answer>; Queens is lower than Brooklyn, so the selected candidate from <hop 1 answer> is Leroy Comrie.
Node 9: Before his election to the NYC Council, <hop 3 answer> served seven years in the office of New York City Council Member Archie Spigner.
Hop result: the requested count is 7.
